PermissionResultAllow.signature: (self, behavior: Literal['allow'] = 'allow', updated_input: dict[str, Any] | None = None, updated_permissions: list[claude_agent_sdk.types.PermissionUpdate] | None = None) -> None

PermissionResultDeny.signature: (self, behavior: Literal['deny'] = 'deny', message: str = '', interrupt: bool = False) -> None

ToolPermissionContext.signature: (self, signal: Any | None = None, suggestions: list[claude_agent_sdk.types.PermissionUpdate] = <factory>, tool_use_id: str | None = None, agent_id: str | None = None) -> None

Q1_callback_carries_enough.caveat: tool_use_id is Optional[str] on ToolPermissionContext. Adapter must handle None defensively.

Q2_sync_emission_possible.answer: yes — the callback is async and the adapter can emit evidence inside the callback before returning the result

Q3_policy_snapshot_correlation.answer: purely harness-side. The SDK provides no 'which policy was in effect' context. Same pattern as other lanes; not a blocker.
